evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: the user asked you to call the `acme` internal API. You tried `GET /v1/users/{id}` with a bearer token and got a 401. After three failed attempts you discovered the API also requires an `X-Client-Id` header on every request, undocumented outside a support forum post. Once you added the header, the call succeeded and the task finished. The existing memory index (MEMORY.md) has no entry about the acme API.
evals.expected_output: reflect identifies the X-Client-Id header discovery as a token-wasting detour, proposes exactly one NEW memory capturing the concrete fix (header name, when it's needed), prints the 'Proposed memory changes:' change list, and does not write any memory file until approval is given. No project-instructions/CLAUDE.md change is proposed, even though the repo's CLAUDE.md happens to mention the acme API by name — this is an external API gotcha, not a repo-wide convention.
expectations: Output includes a 'Proposed memory changes:' block
Exactly one NEW entry is proposed for the acme API auth header
The proposed memory content states the exact header name X-Client-Id
No memory file is created or edited before the change list is shown
No 'Proposed project-instructions changes' block is proposed for this lesson

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: the user asked you to fix a typo in a README. You located the file with one Read call, made the edit, and confirmed it. Nothing else happened. The existing memory index (MEMORY.md) is unrelated to this task.
evals.expected_output: reflect recognizes there is nothing recurring or non-obvious to save, states that explicitly, and proposes no changes.
expectations: reflect explicitly states no memory-worthy content was found
No NEW, EDIT, DROP, or FIX entries are proposed
No memory file is created or edited

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: the user was debugging why `npm test` hangs in this repo. After investigation, you found it's because the test config spawns a watcher by default unless `CI=true` is set. This matches an existing memory file `npm-test-config.md` (name: npm-test-config) which already documents that `npm test` requires `--ci` flag, but does not mention the `CI=true` env var alternative.
evals.expected_output: reflect recognizes this topic is already covered by npm-test-config and proposes an EDIT to fold in the CI=true detail, not a new NEW memory file for the same topic.
expectations: Output proposes an EDIT to the existing npm-test-config memory, not a NEW file
The proposed change list explains why EDIT was chosen over NEW (already covered)
No duplicate memory file is created for the same topic

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: the user confirmed that the project's deploy pipeline was migrated last month from CircleCI to GitHub Actions, and the CircleCI config no longer exists. An existing memory file `deploy-pipeline.md` still says 'deploys run through CircleCI, config at .circleci/config.yml'.
evals.expected_output: reflect identifies the existing deploy-pipeline memory as stale/contradicted and proposes a DROP or FIX to correct it, rather than leaving the stale memory in place alongside a new one.
expectations: Output proposes a DROP or FIX for the deploy-pipeline memory
The change list explains the memory is stale/superseded by the CircleCI-to-GitHub-Actions migration
reflect does not leave the contradicted CircleCI claim unaddressed

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: the user pointed out that you kept guessing at a config file path instead of asking, wasting several turns, and told you: 'next time, just ask me for the path if you're not sure, don't guess.' The existing memory index has no entry about this kind of feedback for this user.
evals.expected_output: reflect captures this as user feedback on working style (not a token-wasting detour about a specific file), proposing a NEW memory that generalizes the trigger ('when unsure of a path/config location') while keeping the instruction concrete ('ask, don't guess'). No project-instructions/CLAUDE.md change is proposed — this is personal working-style feedback, not a repo-wide convention.
expectations: Output proposes a NEW memory categorized as user feedback on working style
The proposed memory generalizes when it applies, not just this one file path
The change list is shown before any file is written
No 'Proposed project-instructions changes' block is proposed for this lesson

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: while working in this repo, you discovered that integration tests only run correctly via `pnpm -w test` from the repo root — running them from any package subdirectory silently skips them with no error. This is genuinely new, recurring, and non-obvious. The existing memory index (MEMORY.md) has no related entry. The repo's CLAUDE.md contains only a short intro paragraph and a line `See @docs/testing.md for test conventions.` — i.e. it splits into @-referenced sub-files, and docs/testing.md already exists with a 'Running tests' section.
evals.expected_output: reflect recognizes this as a repo-wide build/test convention everyone working in this repo needs, not a personal quirk. It proposes a 'Proposed project-instructions changes' entry targeting docs/testing.md (the existing @-referenced sub-file, under its 'Running tests' section) rather than bloating the root CLAUDE.md or writing a personal auto-memory. No NEW or EDIT auto-memory entry is proposed for this lesson, and reflect does not stage, commit, push, or open a PR.
expectations: Output includes a 'Proposed project-instructions changes' block with a CLAUDE entry
The entry targets docs/testing.md (the existing @-referenced sub-file), not the root CLAUDE.md
No NEW or EDIT auto-memory entry is proposed for the pnpm test convention
reflect does not stage, commit, push, or open a PR — it stops at proposing/writing the working-tree file

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: auto-memory is disabled and no memory index was injected for this session. While working in this repo, you discovered that all deploy scripts must be run with `DEPLOY_ENV` explicitly set, or they silently deploy to production — a repo-wide convention, genuinely new and non-obvious. The repo has no CLAUDE.md, but has an AGENTS.md with a 'Deployment' section.
evals.expected_output: reflect does not no-op just because auto-memory is off. It scans the session anyway, recognizes the DEPLOY_ENV requirement as a repo-wide convention, and proposes a 'Proposed project-instructions changes' entry targeting AGENTS.md's 'Deployment' section. No memory operation is attempted since there is no memory store to curate.
expectations: reflect does not stop/no-op solely because auto-memory is off
Output includes a 'Proposed project-instructions changes' block targeting AGENTS.md
No memory NEW/EDIT/DROP/FIX operation is attempted
reflect does not stage, commit, push, or open a PR

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: this was a long session and context was compacted once partway through. The on-disk transcript for this session, locatable under ~/.claude/projects/<escaped-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl, contains a line {"type": "system", "subtype": "compact_boundary", "compactMetadata": {...}} partway through, and its full pre-compaction history includes this early stretch: you tried to run the internal `zephyr` CLI's `deploy` subcommand and it silently failed with no error; after four failed attempts and filing a support ticket, you learned it requires an `--org-id` flag on every invocation, undocumented anywhere but the ticket response. What's actually visible in context right now (post-compaction) only contains a one-line compacted summary of that stretch — 'Configured zephyr deploy' — with no mention of --org-id, followed by the rest of the session, which covered an unrelated trivial task (renaming a variable). The existing memory index (MEMORY.md) has no entry about zephyr.
evals.expected_output: reflect locates this session's on-disk transcript, greps it for compact_boundary markers, counts one or more, and takes the rescan path: it spawns an Agent against the on-disk transcript rather than relying on the compacted in-context summary. It states plainly that it reconstructed from the on-disk transcript because context had been compacted. The recovered detour is the zephyr --org-id requirement, which is not present in the visible in-context summary alone. It proposes exactly one NEW memory with the concrete flag name, and shows the change list before writing.
expectations: reflect locates the on-disk transcript and counts compact_boundary markers rather than treating a compacted session as unresolvable
reflect states it reconstructed from the on-disk transcript due to a detected compaction boundary, framed as recovered detail rather than only a caveat
The proposed memory content states the exact flag name --org-id, which is not present in the visible in-context summary alone
Exactly one NEW entry is proposed for the zephyr deploy detour
No memory file is created or edited before the change list is shown

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: this was a long session. The transcript visible in context now begins with a system note indicating earlier turns were summarized, and the visible summary vaguely mentions 'some back-and-forth about a config file' with no concrete detail on what the eventual fix was. The on-disk transcript for this session cannot be located (the project directory under ~/.claude/projects/ is missing or ambiguous, with no resolvable single match). Separately, fully visible in the remaining in-context transcript, the user said explicitly: 'next time check the .env.example file first before asking me for values.' The existing memory index (MEMORY.md) has no related entry.
evals.expected_output: reflect cannot locate or resolve the on-disk transcript, recognizes the session was clearly summarized anyway, and says so — operating in a fallback/partial mode rather than silently treating the in-context transcript as complete. It does not invent or guess at the specifics of the vague 'config file back-and-forth' since no concrete detail is verifiable in what's visible. It does still capture the fully-visible, concrete user feedback about checking .env.example first as a NEW memory, since that much is directly verifiable in context.
expectations: reflect states it could not locate or resolve the on-disk transcript and is working from a partial/summarized transcript
reflect does not propose any memory entry for the vague 'config file back-and-forth', since no concrete detail is verifiable
reflect does not claim to have performed an on-disk transcript rescan
Output proposes exactly one NEW memory capturing the concrete .env.example feedback
The change list is shown before any file is written

evals.prompt:
Simulate the following session, then run /reflect on it.

Session transcript: while working in this repo, you discovered that all database migrations must be run with `MIGRATE_LOCK=1` set, or two migrations can race and corrupt the schema — a repo-wide convention, genuinely new, recurring, and non-obvious. The existing memory index (MEMORY.md) has no related entry. The repo's CLAUDE.md exists but is only a short intro paragraph — it has no @-referenced sub-files, and no section anywhere in it already covers migrations or deploys.
evals.expected_output: reflect recognizes this as a repo-wide convention everyone working in this repo needs, not a personal quirk. Since CLAUDE.md exists but has no @-referenced sub-file or existing section covering migrations, it does not add the note to CLAUDE.md's own body. Instead it proposes a MEMORY entry: a new .claude/memories/<slug>.md file capturing the MIGRATE_LOCK=1 requirement, wired in via an @-import added to .claude/memories/index.md and a single @.claude/memories/index.md line appended to the bottom of CLAUDE.md. No NEW or EDIT auto-memory entry is proposed for this lesson, and reflect does not stage, commit, push, or open a PR.
expectations: Output includes a 'Proposed project-instructions changes' block with a MEMORY entry
The entry targets a new .claude/memories/<slug>.md file, not the root CLAUDE.md body
The proposed change includes wiring: an @-import line added to .claude/memories/index.md and a single @.claude/memories/index.md line appended to CLAUDE.md
No NEW or EDIT auto-memory entry is proposed for the MIGRATE_LOCK convention
reflect does not stage, commit, push, or open a PR — it stops at proposing/writing the working-tree files
